signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and stable, but the board is still no-trade, funding is positive, and the live veto argues against opening fresh exposure. Neutral is still the right stance.
ETH is mildly constructive but still low-edge, with positive funding and a neutral board read. Not enough asymmetry to justify a new position.
SOL has slightly better funding support than ETH, but the board remains no-trade and the active veto keeps it in stand-down territory.
TAO is oversold with strongly negative funding and real liquidity, which makes it interesting, but the board is still mixed rather than clearly reversal-ready. Watchlist only under veto conditions.
WLFI has some reversal ingredients with negative funding, crowded positioning, and good enough liquidity, but the board is still not clean enough to justify fresh risk while veto is active.
TNSR still screens as the strongest continuation long in the set with strong momentum and negative funding, but the live veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta continuation exposure.
ZEREBRO has momentum, but funding is positive, liquidity is thin, and the board is still too mixed to trust into a veto regime.
ARB has acceptable liquidity and mild strength, but the board remains neutral and funding is positive. Interesting, not actionable.

KAITO is the clearest bearish lean on the board with a sharp negative 24h move, positive funding, and a bearish mechanical profile. It is still thin, so conviction stays moderate rather than aggressive.
CHILLGUY is moving hard, but the market is too thin, too wide, and too funding-rich on the long side to trust as a fresh continuation trade.
BLUR still screens as a valid squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the live veto is the wrong environment to chase it. Strong watchlist long, not an active trade.
